title: Federal Reserve says U.S. banks can withstand $708 billion in losses amid overhaul of capital rules
description: The Fed's annual exercise comes at a pivotal moment for bank regulation because, unlike previous years, the results will not affect capital requirements.

title: Kalshi CEO says prediction market thinking about IPO, but not for this year
description: Tarek Mansour said to CNBC's "Squawk Box" it made sense for the company at the stage to think about a debut on public markets.

title: New meme stock Wendy's soars more than 25% with trading halted at one point
description: While management changes can influence investor sentiment, the magnitude of the move suggests other forces may be at play.

title: CFTC sues Kentucky over prediction markets enforcement actions; first 'red state' to face such  lawsuit
description: The commission has now sued nine states in its battle to defend what it sees as its exclusive right to regulate event contracts.

title: Mamdani-backed candidates are likely to win in NYC primaries, prediction market traders expect
description: Voters are heading to the polls in New York, with four competitive races in Democratic congressional primaries in focus.

title: SpaceX seeing some interest from short sellers, but many still afraid to bet against Musk
description: About 40 million SpaceX shares are sold short, roughly 5% to 7% of the shares that are publicly available to trade, according to an estimate by S3 Partners.

title: Meta is building a prediction markets app. These stocks fell in response
description: The report said that CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed staff to develop a prediction markets platform, internally known as "Arena."

title: U.S. fights with Brazil for China's giant soybean market
description: The U.S. Soybean Export Council makes claims to higher quality a selling point over Brazil's crop, as American farmers attempt to win back business from the world's largest importer

title: China's 618 shopping festival growth slows sharply as consumer spending malaise persists
description: The latest consumer data reinforce how consumption in China remains subdued.

title: Can anyone join Musk in the trillionaire club? Zuckerberg has best shot, according to prediction markets
description: The Meta CEO is seen as next to join the Tesla and SpaceX CEO. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is seen as the second-most-likely.

title: Alan Greenspan, former chairman of the Fed, dies at age 100
description: Alan Greenspan presided over the Federal Reserve for 19 years under four presidents and mastered the art of obfuscation known as Fedspeak.

title: 'Regime change but in a velvet glove': How Kevin Warsh has set out to remake the Fed
description: The first big announced changes point toward a quiet revolution, with task forces set up to rethink virtually everything done at the Fed.

title: U.S. and Iran begin peace talks amid conflicting claims over Strait of Hormuz
description: Iran's military said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to continued Israeli military strikes in Lebanon; the U.S. said it remains open.

title: Bitcoin's future as revolutionary as the smartphone, according to CoinDesk
description: CoinDesk’s president of indices and data has a message for investors: Don’t count out bitcoin.

title: The riskiest SpaceX stock trade of all had a big first week
description: SpaceX's IPO did not just mint the biggest debut in market history and a trillionaire, but it also triggered a historic land grab in risky leveraged ETFs.

title: The average SpaceX buyer post-IPO is almost under water after two-day slide
description: Shares of SpaceX fell as much as 7% Thursday to $178, leaving the stock trading roughly in line with its volume-weighted average price of just under $180.

title: Markets are set for a much more hawkish Warsh Fed than expected
description: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh's tough talk on inflation Wednesday reverberated through financial markets.

title: Jeffrey Gundlach says Fed's Warsh is not going to be the 'easy money' chairman many hoped for
description: Gundlach said Warsh's stance reduces the risk of overly accommodative monetary policy that could reignite inflation and push longer-term borrowing costs higher.

title: Chairman Warsh abstains from giving rate forecast as several members signal a hike in 2026
description: The median projection called for the federal funds rate to end 2026 at 3.8%, a quarter percentage point above the current target range.

title: Chairman Warsh drastically alters Fed rate statement. Here's what's changed
description: This is a comparison of Wednesday's Federal Open Market Committee statement with the one issued after the Fed's previous policymaking meeting in April.
